perturbation.original_text: TERM: The rights granted hereunder shall be effective as of the Effective Date and shall expire on December 31, 2006 (the "Term"); provided, however, that with respect to each Property, including Robots, Kingdom of Heaven, Mr. and Mrs. Smith, In Her Shoes, Idiocracy (Oww My Balls) and Ice Age II, all right and licenses granted herein will continue in full force and effect until March 31, 2008.
perturbation.changed_text: TERM: The rights granted hereunder shall be effective as of the Effective Date and shall expire on December 31, 2006 (the "Term"); provided, however, that with respect to each Property, including Robots, Kingdom of Heaven, Mr. and Mrs. Smith, In Her Shoes, Idiocracy (Oww My Balls) and Ice Age II, all right and licenses granted herein will continue in full force and effect until March 31, 2008, unless Fox provides written notice of termination at least 30 days prior to December 31, 2006.
perturbation.explanation: By adding the clause 'unless Fox provides written notice of termination at least 30 days prior to December 31, 2006,' we introduce an ambiguity. The original text states the agreement continues until March 31, 2008, for specific properties. Now, the modified text allows Fox to terminate the agreement earlier with a 30-day notice, creating an in-text contradiction. The contradiction directly impacts the licensee, as it introduces uncertainty on the term length. The modified text now directly contradicts what is implied and explicitly stated about the continuation of the agreement till March 31, 2008.
perturbation.contradicted_text: TERM: The rights granted hereunder shall be effective as of the Effective Date and shall expire on December 31, 2006 (the "Term"); provided, however, that with respect to each Property, including Robots, Kingdom of Heaven, Mr. and Mrs. Smith, In Her Shoes, Idiocracy (Oww My Balls) and Ice Age II, all right and licenses granted herein will continue in full force and effect until March 31, 2008, unless Fox provides written notice of termination at least 30 days prior to December 31, 2006.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction by allowing Fox to terminate the agreement earlier with a 30-day notice, even though the original text states the agreement continues until March 31, 2008, for specific properties. This creates an in-text contradiction as the licensee now has uncertainty on the term length. The added clause, 'unless Fox provides written notice of termination at least 30 days prior to December 31, 2006,' directly contradicts the implied and explicitly stated continuation of the agreement till March 31, 2008. This aligns with the definition of Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction, because the key term 'Term' is inconsistently defined, making enforcement unclear.

perturbation.original_text: NOTICE PROVISION: The notice information for Licensee in Section 17(a) of the Agreement shall be amended such that "Paul Zuzelo" is deleted and replaced with "General Counsel", and such that the email address for Paul Zuzelo is deleted.
perturbation.changed_text: NOTICE PROVISION: The notice information for Licensee in Section 17(a) of the Agreement shall be amended such that "Paul Zuzelo" is deleted and replaced with "General Counsel", and such that the email address for Paul Zuzelo is deleted. However, all legal notices must also be sent to Paul Zuzelo at his last known email address to be considered valid.
perturbation.explanation: The original text removes Paul Zuzelo as the point of contact for notices. The added clause now states that all legal notices *must* also be sent to Paul Zuzelo to be valid, creating a contradiction. Now, legal notices must be sent to two recipients, one official (General Counsel) and one that was removed, to be valid, which creates an in-text contradiction. The direct contradiction impacts the effectiveness of the notice provision as intended in section 17(a) of the original agreement, since is not clear now where the notice should be delivered.
perturbation.contradicted_text: NOTICE PROVISION: The notice information for Licensee in Section 17(a) of the Agreement shall be amended such that "Paul Zuzelo" is deleted and replaced with "General Counsel", and such that the email address for Paul Zuzelo is deleted. However, all legal notices must also be sent to Paul Zuzelo at his last known email address to be considered valid.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts itself. The original text in Section 17(a) removes Paul Zuzelo as a point of contact. However, the changed text adds a clause stating that legal notices *must* also be sent to Paul Zuzelo to be considered valid. This creates a direct conflict, as the agreement now requires sending notices to someone who was explicitly removed as the point of contact, creating a contradiction within the text, and making it ambiguous where the notice should be delivered.
